homePage.heroTitle: Unified Digital Command Center for Medical Discharge and Legal Acknowledgment
homePage.heroSubtitle: An enterprise-grade platform that connects clinical, legal, and patient affairs teams in a governed digital framework to document discharge decisions, manage refusal cases, and execute legal escalation with full compliance assurance.
homePage.whatTitle: WathiqCare: Digital Governance for Healthcare Institutions
homePage.whatBody: An integrated operating system designed to manage the full lifecycle of medical discharge decisions—from clinical judgment to legal documentation and regulatory escalation—providing an immutable audit trail that protects rights and supports institutional compliance.
homePage.coreModulesSubtitle: Integrated platform covering all aspects of medico-legal governance

hero.title: Medical Discharge Refusal Management Platform
hero.subtitle: WathiqCare — an integrated system that helps healthcare facilities document discharge refusals, manage legal escalation, and ensure regulatory compliance with speed and security.

features.title: Platform Features
features.subtitle: Everything a healthcare facility needs to manage discharge refusal cases legally and with full documentation

workflow.title: Clinical Workflow Management
workflow.desc: Record medical discharge orders, track patient refusals, and validate ICD-11 codes in real time.

legal.title: Automated Legal Escalation
legal.desc: Automatically generate legal case files with escalation timelines at 24 / 48 / 72 hours.

forms.title: Digital Refusal Forms
forms.desc: Discharge refusal forms with witness attestation and documented electronic signature.

dashboard.desc: Key performance indicators, compliance reports, and discharge refusal case statistics.

security.title: Security & PDPL Compliance
security.desc: Immutable audit log, data encryption, and full compliance with Saudi Personal Data Protection Law.

howItWorks.title: How the System Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Four simple steps from registration to legal archiving

step1.title: Case Registration
step1.desc: The physician enters the medical discharge order and records the patient's refusal directly in the system.

step2.title: Document Generation
step2.desc: The system instantly generates a discharge refusal form with electronic signature and witness confirmation.

step3.title: Legal Escalation
step3.desc: If the refusal persists, the system triggers the automated legal escalation cycle with team notifications.

step4.title: Archiving & Reporting
step4.desc: All events are stored in a secure, auditable log accessible for review at any time.

clinical.title: Physicians & Clinical Teams
clinical.desc: Instant documentation of discharge orders and patient responses without administrative burden.

facility.desc: Full legal protection, precise reporting, and automatic compliance with health regulations.

privacyPage.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPage.subtitle: Effective Date: January 1, 2025
privacyPage.intro: WathiqCare is committed to protecting the privacy and confidentiality of all personal health data processed through this platform. This policy describes how we collect, use, store, and safeguard your information in accordance with the Saudi Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) and applicable healthcare regulations.

sections.heading: 1. Data We Collect
sections.body: We collect patient health information, clinical documentation, staff authentication credentials, and facility configuration data strictly as necessary to operate the discharge refusal management workflow.

sections.heading: 2. How We Use Your Data
sections.body: All collected data is used exclusively to (a) generate legally required medical discharge documentation, (b) maintain compliance audit trails, (c) support legal escalation workflows, and (d) provide platform analytics to authorized administrators.

sections.heading: 3. Data Storage & Security
sections.body: Data is encrypted at rest using AES-256 and in transit using TLS 1.3. All data is stored in GDPR and PDPL compliant cloud infrastructure. Access is role-based and fully audited.

sections.heading: 4. Data Retention
sections.body: Medical records and audit logs are retained for the minimum period required by Saudi MOH regulations and applicable healthcare law. Tenants may request data deletion after the mandatory retention period expires.

sections.heading: 5. Your Rights
sections.body: Under the PDPL, you have the right to access, correct, and request deletion of your personal data. Requests may be submitted to privacy@wathiqcare.online.

sections.heading: 6. Contact
sections.body: For privacy inquiries, contact our Data Protection Officer at: privacy@wathiqcare.online

termsPage.title: Terms of Use
termsPage.subtitle: Effective Date: January 1, 2025
termsPage.intro: By accessing or using the WathiqCare platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Use. If you do not agree, please do not use the platform.

sections.heading: 1. Authorized Use
sections.body: WathiqCare is a B2B medico-legal workflow platform licensed exclusively to authorized healthcare facilities. Access is restricted to credentialed clinical, administrative, and legal staff of licensed tenants.

sections.heading: 2. Data Responsibility
sections.body: Tenant administrators are responsible for ensuring that all patient data entered into the platform is accurate, authorized, and handled in accordance with applicable healthcare privacy laws including the Saudi PDPL.

sections.heading: 3. Prohibited Activities
sections.body: You may not reverse-engineer the platform, attempt unauthorized access, use the platform for any purpose other than licensed medical documentation, or share access credentials with unauthorized parties.

sections.heading: 4. Limitation of Liability
sections.body: WathiqCare provides workflow tools to support clinical and legal documentation. All medical, legal, and clinical decisions remain the sole responsibility of the licensed healthcare professional and facility.

sections.heading: 5. Modifications
sections.body: We reserve the right to update these terms at any time. Continued use of the platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

sections.heading: 6. Governing Law
sections.body: These Terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. Any disputes will be subject to the jurisdiction of Saudi courts.
